rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA CZECH et al VS. CERTAIN-TEED CORPORTION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike Punitive Damages Claim In Plaintiffs' Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 23, 2008, line 1. DEFENDANT DURABLA MANUFACTURING COMPANY'S Motion To Strike Punitive Damages Claim In Plaintiffs' Complaint is denied. The allegations are sufficient under Perkins v. Superior Court (1982) 31 Cal. 3d 147, 158; Civil Code Section 3294. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: ISMAEL MAYEN VS. MEREDITH RICHARDSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 23, 2008, line 2. DEFENDANT MEREDITH RICHARDSON'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. rulings.Case Title: JULIE WANG VS. JOANNE M REMING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Prefiling Order Prohibiting Vexatious Litigant From Filing New Litigation In Propria Persona And Order Requiring Pltf To Post Security Deposit
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 23, 2008, line 4. DEFENDANTS STIMMEL, STIMMEL & SMITH, PC, LEE STIMMEL AND ANDRINE SMITH'S Motion For Prefiling Order Prohibiting Vexatious Litigant From Filing New Litigation In Propria Persona And Order Requiring Plaintiff To Post Security Deposit is granted. The record supports a finding that plaintiff is a vexatious litigant under CCP section 391.1(b)(3). Plaintiff is required to post a bond in the amount of $3,000.00 on or before October 30, 2008. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: DEBORA MARTIN VS. SERGIO IANTORNO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Attorney'S Fees Following Granting Of Special Motion To Strike Various Causes Of Action In Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 23, 2008, line 8. DEFENDANTS SERGIO IANTORNO AND GOLDEN PROPERTIES, LLC'S Motion For Attorney's Fees Following Granting Of Special Motion To Strike Various Causes Of Action In Second Amended Complaint. The motion is granted and the moving parties are awarded $24,892.50 in fees. The rates are reasonable on plaintiff has failed to demonstrate that defendants are not entitled to fees. The motion is timely filed and plaintiff's request for a continuance is denied for plaintiff did not seek a continuance by ex parte motion. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: U.S. BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION VS. VATIMA PATTON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 23, 2008, line 18. PLAINTIFF US BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Plaintiff has not shown that defendants were given notice and have not shown good cause under Gross v. Superior Court (1985) 171 Cal. App. 3d 265. There has been no showing that the property is not subject to the Rent Ordinance. Plaintiff also has not shown that damages are undisputed. =(302/PJM)
